name: 19 · Postgres Node
description: Demo of the Postgres executor backend. Start collects {min_id, new_name}; a Postgres READ step selects rows from the demo `demo_pg.widgets` table where `id >= $1` (param borrowed from `{{ start.min_id }}`, projection `[id, name]`); a Postgres WRITE step then INSERTs a new widget with `RETURNING id, name` (params `{{ start.new_name }}`). End maps the read rows/row_count plus the write's rows_affected and the RETURNING row. Both steps bind the workspace `postgres` resource `demo_pg`, pointed at the dev Postgres (host: localhost, port: 15439, db: mekhan, user/pw: mekhan — matches `just dev` slot-0). The connection (host/port/database/username/password/sslmode) is overlaid via ResourceChannel::ConfigOverlay; the backend builds/caches a sqlx PgPool keyed by connection identity. IMPORTANT: this never touches mekhan's own tables — it reads/writes a dedicated `demo_pg.widgets` fixture table. Seed it once with `just dev pg-demo-seed` (applies demos/19-postgres-node/seed.sql), then publish. Requires a `postgres` resource named `demo_pg` in the workspace at /resources (auto-seeded from demos/resources/demo_pg.json).
